Conduct commissioning verification sampling and produce a compliance demonstration report suitable for submission to the POTW.
payload.sizingAndPricing: Indicative capital range: $80K–$220K (MEDIUM-LOW confidence). The primary driver of range is facility hydraulic loading, which has not yet been confirmed. Secondary drivers include site space constraints, existing civil/plumbing leverage, and separator technology selection (CPI vs. high-efficiency gravity). These variables will be quantified during a site visit and preliminary engineering assessment. No firm pricing is committed in this document. The sizing range and confidence tier will be updated following site assessment. Phase-2 recurring service (compliance monitoring and O&G trend reporting): $15K–$30K/year — separate engagement, scoped and priced after commissioning.
payload.schedule: Indicative schedule pending site visit confirmation. Site visit and flow measurement: within 2 weeks of engagement commitment. Preliminary sizing and equipment selection: 2–3 weeks following site visit. Engineering design and permitting (if required by local jurisdiction): 4–8 weeks. Equipment procurement and installation: 6–12 weeks. Commissioning and verification sampling: 2–4 weeks post-installation. Total indicative timeline from commitment to commissioning: approximately 14–27 weeks. Note: schedule will be refined and calibrated against the timing of the next POTW monitoring event.

commitments.label: Scope delivery
commitments.text: Following site visit and flow confirmation, we will deliver a formal scoping proposal with firm capex range, equipment selection, installation schedule, and commissioning plan — calibrated against the timing of the next POTW monitoring event.

commitments.label: Preliminary sizing
commitments.text: A preliminary sizing range and indicative installation schedule will be delivered within 2–3 weeks of the site visit. This will form the basis for any internal capital approval process at the facility level.

commitments.label: No commitment to regulatory outcome
commitments.text: This engagement produces a technically sound compliant pretreatment system. Compliance demonstration will be supported by commissioning verification sampling and a POTW-ready documentation package. Regulatory outcome (POTW acceptance, formal compliance schedule withdrawal) is not guaranteed.

payload.statusOfDocument: DRAFT INTENT — for internal use only; not for external distribution. To be revised by the field agent and proposal team following site visit and flow confirmation.

sizingRows.label: Primary range driver
sizingRows.value: Facility hydraulic loading across 3 washer-extractor lines (not yet measured)

sizingRows.label: Secondary range drivers
sizingRows.value: Site space constraints, civil/plumbing reuse, separator technology selection

sizingRows.label: Confidence tier
sizingRows.value: MEDIUM-LOW — will update to MEDIUM or HIGH following site assessment

outOfScope.heading: Regulatory negotiations with the POTW
outOfScope.body: This scope does not include formal representation in POTW enforcement proceedings, negotiation of compliance schedule milestones, or any regulatory outcome guarantee. The scope produces a technically sound compliant system; regulatory acceptance is the customer's responsibility.

outOfScope.heading: Assessment of non-O&G discharge parameters
outOfScope.body: This scope addresses oil-and-grease exceedances only. Any other POTW discharge parameters (nutrients, metals, solvents, PFAS, or other local limits) are out of scope unless specifically added following a full pretreatment assessment.

outOfScope.heading: Laundry process chemistry modifications
outOfScope.body: Modifications to detergent formulations, solvent use, or laundry process chemistry are not included in this scope. If process chemistry changes are recommended following site assessment, they would be scoped separately.

outOfScope.heading: Firm pricing or contract commitment
outOfScope.body: This document is a scoping intent outline only. No firm pricing, contractual commitment, or binding schedule is established herein. A formal proposal will follow site assessment and flow confirmation.

gatesToClose.gate: Facility hydraulic loading (gal/day) and O&G mass loading confirmed across all 3 washer-extractor lines
gatesToClose.closer: Site visit — field agent + plant manager

gatesToClose.gate: Site space constraints and existing civil/plumbing configuration assessed
Timing of next POTW monitoring event confirmed
Regional ops VP awareness confirmed and approval pathway for >$150K capital mapped
gatesToClose.closer: Plant manager + field agent first call

gatesToClose.gate: Customer commitment to proceed to scoping / site visit secured

finalText:
All four artifacts are ready. Here's the full package.

---

## Package Ready — Industrial Uniform Laundry Facility, NC | **Qualify Stage**

**Bottom line:** Their cheap path — cleaning or replacing the trap in-kind — doesn't resolve the structural capacity mismatch. Fully priced over five years it runs **~$140K–$370K plus enforcement tail risk**. A right-sized engineered system comes in at **~$80K–$220K with compliance risk permanently extinguished**. That's the executive sentence you walk in with.

---

### ⛔ STOP-01 — Third Exceedance Imminent
Two violation notices in six months. POTW has explicitly threatened a formal compliance schedule with mandatory monthly self-monitoring on the next exceedance. The voluntary resolution window is open right now — and it won't stay open.

---

### Your Four-Artifact Package

| Artifact | File | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Field Brief** | [Download PDF](?threadId=eval-happy-path-industrial-laundry-fog-cX_T3yQOgMVy3d2v-K-RH&kind=field-brief) | Read before the call. Strategic decision aid. |
| **Conversation Playbook** | [Download PDF](?threadId=eval-happy-path-industrial-laundry-fog-cX_T3yQOgMVy3d2v-K-RH&kind=playbook) | Have open during the call. 11 themed question sets. |
| **Analytical Read** | [Download PDF](?threadId=eval-happy-path-industrial-laundry-fog-cX_T3yQOgMVy3d2v-K-RH&kind=analytical-read) | Send to your manager. Evidence-tagged write-up. |
| **Proposal Shell** | [Download PDF](?threadId=eval-happy-path-industrial-laundry-fog-cX_T3yQOgMVy3d2v-K-RH&kind=proposal-shell) | Draft intent only — edit before sharing externally. |

---

### What the Field Brief will tell you to do (top 3 actions)

1. **Book the site visit this week (≤7 days).** Call the plant manager and open with the reframe: *"Your trap was designed for the 1990s footprint — two additional lines means the design basis is wrong, not just the equipment."* Get a site visit date before the next POTW monitoring event. Flows and space constraints are the only things standing between MEDIUM-LOW and MEDIUM confidence on sizing.

2. **Confirm VP awareness and the approval pathway (7–14 days).** Ask directly: *"Is your regional ops VP already aware of the two notices?"* If not, offer to prepare a one-page executive summary the plant manager can use to get ahead of it internally. A deal above $150K stalls without VP pre-engagement — and the right-sized solution probably lands in that territory.

3. **Deliver a preliminary sizing range with a compliance timeline (14–21 days).** After the site visit, come back with a rough capex range and an installation schedule showing the system can be commissioned before the next high-risk monitoring period. That's what converts Qualify to Scope and gives the plant manager something concrete for internal approval.

---

### The framing battle to win

**Land this before anything else:** *"This isn't a maintenance failure — it's a structural capacity mismatch. You've added two lines since the 1990s. Cleaning it more often can't fix a design-basis problem, and the next exceedance puts you on a compliance schedule you can't control the timeline of."*

**The anti-frame to defeat:** *"We just need a bigger trap — we can source that ourselves."* If that framing wins, you lose the engineering value-add entirely.
